Paper_Metadata.Paper_Title: A high strength Al–Li alloy produced by laser powder bed fusion: Densification, microstructure, and mechanical properties

Composition_Info.Note: Gas-atomized powder supplied by Intelligence Manufacturing Technology; mean particle size 35.3 μm; balance Al calculated as 100 - (Cu+Li+Mg+Ag+Zr) = 93.78 wt%

Process_Text.original: Gas atomization process; raw powder particles exhibit nearly spherical shape and have a mean particle size of 35.3 μm.
Process_Text.simplified: Gas-atomized powder, mean particle size 35.3 μm, nearly spherical morphology.

Microstructure_Text.original: SEM: Powder morphology characterized (Fig. 1a); nearly spherical particles observed.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: SEM: Powder morphology characterized (Fig. 1a); nearly spherical particles observed.

Composition_Info.Note: 24% Li loss during LPBF; composition still meets AA2195 standard; balance Al calculated as 100 - (Cu+Li+Mg+Ag+Zr) = 94.20 wt%

Process_Text.original: LPBF on self-developed LPBF machine at room temperature under protective argon atmosphere; optimized parameters: laser power 200 W, hatch spacing 0.1 mm, powder layer thickness 30 μm, laser scanning velocity 80 mm/s, hatch style rotation 90° between consecutive layers; tensile plates (30 mm length × 8 mm width × 2 mm thickness) built perpendicular to building direction; dog-bone samples machined with gauge section 12 mm × 2.5 mm × 1.5 mm.
Process_Text.simplified: LPBF with optimized parameters: 200 W, 80 mm/s, 30 μm layer thickness, 0.1 mm hatch spacing, 90° rotation; relative density 99.9%; crack-free.

Process_Info.Equipment: Self-developed LPBF machine (Huazhong University of Science and Technology)

Microstructure_Text.original: Coarse columnar grain microstructure with epitaxial growth through multiple building layers; MP boundaries and columnar grain boundaries clearly distinguishable; heterogeneous element distribution with Cu and Ag enriched at grain/MP boundaries; Al and Mg show opposite characteristics; three precipitate forms observed: white (Cu-Mg rich), grey (Al2O3 oxide), black (Li-containing δ' phase); granular precipitates inside MP; elongated coarse precipitates at MP boundaries due to slower solidification; low Zr content (<0.16%) unable to provide sufficient Al3Zr nucleation sites. High density of dislocations from rapid cooling (GNDs density 2.13×10^13/m²); KAM analysis shows 13.0% low angle grain boundaries.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Coarse columnar grains (37.9 μm avg); heterogeneous microstructure with Cu/Ag segregation at boundaries; δ' (Al3Li), Al2O3, and eutectic precipitates; high dislocation density 2.13×10^13/m²; FCC Al matrix.

Properties_Info.Note: Reported as 'elongation' in paper; mapped to Elongation_Total

Composition_Info.Note: Same composition as as-fabricated (Li loss already occurred during LPBF); balance Al calculated as 100 - (Cu+Li+Mg+Ag+Zr) = 94.20 wt%

Process_Info.Process_Category: Laser Powder Bed Fusion (LPBF) + Heat Treatment

Process_Text.original: Same LPBF parameters as as-fabricated (laser power 200 W, scanning velocity 80 mm/s, layer thickness 30 μm, hatch spacing 0.1 mm, 90° rotation); T6 heat treatment: solutionized at 520°C for 1 h, immediately quenched with water, aged at 170°C for 6 h, cooled in air to room temperature.
Process_Text.simplified: LPBF (same optimized parameters as as-fabricated) + T6 heat treatment: solution at 520°C/1h, water quench, aging at 170°C/6h, air cool.

Process_Info.Equipment: Self-developed LPBF machine (Huazhong University of Science and Technology)

Microstructure_Text.original: Homogeneous microstructure after T6 treatment; Mg and Ag elements become uniform; Cu-containing particles evenly distributed; coarse columnar grains preserved (34.3 μm avg); T1 (Al2CuLi) plates dominate as strengthening phase, nucleating both at high dislocation density areas and low dislocation density locations; dislocation density slightly decreased but still high (GNDs 1.72×10^13/m²); KAM analysis shows 11.2% LAGB frequency. T1 plates effectively impede dislocation movement.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Homogeneous element distribution after T6; coarse columnar grains (34.3 μm); dominant T1 (Al2CuLi) plates precipitate; high aspect ratio T1 plates provide excellent hardening; dislocation density 1.72×10^13/m²; FCC Al matrix.

Much faster than commercial AA2195-T6 (180 h) due to high dislocation density accelerating T1 precipitation

Composition_Info.Note: Commercial wrought AA2195-T6 plate from literature [36]; composition within AA2195 standard range

Process_Text.original: Commercial wrought AA2195-T6 plate per literature [36]; T6 heat treatment: aging duration 180 hours.
Process_Text.simplified: Commercial wrought AA2195-T6 plate; requires 180 h aging time.

Microstructure_Text.original: Commercial wrought microstructure with T1 (Al2CuLi) precipitates after T6 treatment.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Conventional wrought microstructure with T1 precipitate strengthening.

Properties_Info.Note: From reference [36]; LPBF-T6 exceeds this by ~24 MPa
From reference [36]; LPBF-T6 exceeds this by ~27 MPa

Composition_Info.Note: Commercial wrought AA2195-T8 plate (work-hardened) from literature [36]; requires 2.5% pre-stretch deformation before aging

Process_Text.original: Commercial wrought AA2195-T8 plate per literature [36]; 2.5% deformation before aging; aging duration 20 hours.
Process_Text.simplified: Commercial wrought AA2195-T8 plate with 2.5% pre-stretch; requires 20 h aging time.

Microstructure_Text.original: Conventional wrought microstructure with T1 (Al2CuLi) precipitates and work-hardening effect from 2.5% pre-stretch.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Conventional wrought microstructure with T1 precipitates and work-hardening.

Properties_Info.Note: From reference [36]; LPBF-T6 comparable to this work-hardened condition
From reference [36]; LPBF-T6 exceeds this by ~13 MPa
From reference [36]; LPBF-T6 has slightly lower ductility
